guns.s: No federal firearms license on record for this company in the ATF FFL registry — no licensed firearms dealing or manufacturing.

health.s: 12 CPSC recalls on record; sample hazard: fire & fire-related burn.

topHazards.label: The fan's lithium ion battery can overheat while charging, posing a fire hazard.
The chair frame can bend causing the user to fall, posing a risk of injury.
The chair arms and legs can bend and cause the user to fall, posing a risk of injury.
The plastic blades of the helicopter can detach during operation, posing a laceration hazard.

sampleRecalls.title: Rite Aid Recalls Rechargeable Handheld Fans Due to Fire Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves portable rechargeable handheld fans sold in blue, pink and purple colors. The fans have a one touch button with three speeds and weigh about 8.8 oz. The item number 9050103 can be found on the product box and on a sticker on the backside of the fan itself.
hazards: The fan's lithium ion battery can overheat while charging, posing a fire hazard.
injuries: Rite Aid has received two consumer reports of the rechargeable handheld fans overheating and emitting smoke. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: Rite Aid Recalls Folding Patio Chairs Due to Fall Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves two folding, metal framed patio chairs with padded seats. The chairs were sold in tan and gray. The chairs measure about 24 inches long by 22 inches wide by 37 inches high. UPC number 011822956628 and item number 9043325-MMXIX are printed on the packaging of the tan chair. UPC number 011822952361 …
hazards: The chair frame can bend causing the user to fall, posing a risk of injury.
injuries: The firm has received four reports of chairs bending unexpectedly, including one report of a minor bruise and one laceration injury.

sampleRecalls.title: Rite Aid Recalls Outdoor Dining Sets Due to Fall Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves a six-piece outdoor patio set containing four folding chairs, a table and an umbrella. The brown metal-frame chairs have a red canvas seat and back, and measure 21 ¼ inches tall by 25 ¼ inches wide. The umbrellas are 80 inches tall have a coordinated red with stripes canvas top. The square table a…
hazards: The chair arms and legs can bend and cause the user to fall, posing a risk of injury.
injuries: The firm has received four reports of chairs bending unexpectedly, including four reports of injuries to the lower back and hip.

sampleRecalls.title: Excite USA Recalls Toy Helicopters Due to Laceration Hazard; Sold Exclusively at Rite Aid Stores
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled toy Military Copters are operated by manual rotary starters and sold as a unit with two sizes, 7 and 11 inches in length, of tan and black helicopters. The product comes in a blister pack with "Military Copter with 2 Launchers" marked on the package and "Military Copter" marked on both sides of the manual…
hazards: The plastic blades of the helicopter can detach during operation, posing a laceration hazard.
injuries: Three incidents were reported, two to Excite and one to CPSC. Two of these included laceration injuries.

sampleRecalls.title: Coca-Cola® ElectroPlasma Lamps Sold at Rite Aid Recalled for Burn Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: These lamps are in the shape of a Coca-Cola bottle. They are about 13.5 inches high. The inside of ElectroPlasma lamps simulate lightning bolts. A label on the bottom of the black base of the lamp reads: "Model No: 23-103," and "Lipan Industrial Co., Ltd."
hazards: There is a burn hazard caused by an electric arc between the metal bottle cap and a human contact point. An electric burn is possible if the distance between t…
injuries: The firm has received reports of two incidents involving an electrical arc to the finger, including one report of a minor burn.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee tripped and fell, suffered a broken left hip, and was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to tripping over an object
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee tripped on a plastic tote behind a register, fell to the floor, and suffered a broken left hip. The employee was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level while climbing stairs, steps, or curbs
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee tripped on a step while entering his office. The employee fell on the same level and hit his head resulting in a bruise, and injured his hip. The employee was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slipping
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On February 25, 2020, an employee slipped and fell on ice in a parking lot. The employee suffered fractures to left-side ribs and the left arm.
An employee was reaching for an item on an upper shelf when she fell to the ground, resulting in a leg fracture and head laceration.
